goal: Tarjeta de destino secundario Miami en el homepage de rentatelo.com — evocar reconocimiento instantáneo de Ocean Drive en blue hour, sin personas, neon activado, icónico y aspiracional para cualquier familia LATAM.

creative_direction.core_idea: Ocean Drive en blue hour — el Miami que todos reconocen, sin personas
creative_direction.visual_thesis: Esta tarjeta debe hacer que cualquier LATAM diga 'eso es Miami' en 1 segundo — reconocimiento cultural inmediato, no turismo genérico

composition.layout: Art Deco building facades filling left two-thirds, royal palms in center median, street receding into right distance, wet pavement reflections anchoring foreground

camera.framing: 4:3 destination card — buildings fill upper 70%, wet pavement reflection in lower 30%
camera.angle: eye-level from street, slightly below sign height to capture neon vertical signage
camera.depth_of_field: deep — full facade sharp from foreground to mid-block, atmospheric softness at far end of street

overlay_compatibility.overlay_zone: lower-left corner and potential label badge
overlay_compatibility.zone_content: wet dark pavement with neon reflections — dark enough for text

buildings: Hotel Breakwater — iconic vertical neon sign activated
Colony Hotel — pastel yellow facade, neon script sign
Carlyle Hotel — Art Deco three-tower facade
subject.facade_colors: Art Deco pastels — pink, turquoise, pale yellow, coral — muted by blue hour ambient but warmed by neon glow
subject.palms: tall royal palms in Ocean Drive center median, fronds catching neon color
subject.vehicle: classic American car silhouette parked at curb — 1950s or 1960s, no brand visible, dark color, wet roof reflecting neon
subject.street: wet pavement from recent rain, horizontal neon reflections creating color streaks

scene.location: Ocean Drive, South Beach, Miami Beach, FL — 5th to 10th Street block, looking north
scene.time_of_day: blue hour — 15 to 25 minutes after sunset, neon fully activated, sky deep indigo-purple
environment: Art Deco building facades with pastel paint and activated neon vertical signs
tall royal palms in center median, 30–40 feet height
wet pavement from recent light rain — active neon reflections
deep indigo-purple post-sunset sky above rooflines
classic car silhouette parked at street curb
scene.background_view: Ocean Drive receding into mid-distance, consistent Art Deco facades both sides, deep indigo sky as ceiling, no ocean or beach visible from this angle
critical_scene_rules: zero people — street is completely empty of pedestrians and moving traffic
sky must be deep indigo-blue post-sunset — not black night, not warm golden hour
neon signs must be activated and contributing warm pink, red, and amber color to scene
wet pavement is mandatory — creates iconic neon reflections on street surface
classic car silhouette at curb is optional but preferred for Miami cultural cue
no beach, no ocean, no sand visible in this shot
buildings must be clearly Art Deco South Beach — not generic Miami high-rises

lighting.primary: blue hour ambient — deep indigo sky providing cool base light on facades
lighting.neon_contribution: warm pink, red, amber neon glow from building signs — primary warm light source at street level
lighting.palm_lighting: neon color spill tinting fronds warm pink or amber
lighting.pavement: wet surface acting as mirror for neon signs — horizontal color streaks of pink and amber
lighting.quality: high contrast between cool indigo sky and warm neon street level — defining blue hour tension

dominant: neon pink and coral on facades
accent: teal 168F8A in shadowed facade areas and palm base
rules: sky strictly deep indigo-blue — no warm tones above rooflines
neon glow must be warm pink-amber — no cold blue neon
pavement must show clear neon reflections — not dry or matte
Art Deco pastels visible under blue hour ambient but secondary to neon warmth
overall image: cool sky tension with warm neon street level — this contrast is the Miami signature

negative_prompt: moving vehicles or headlights in motion
generic Miami skyline or Biscayne Bay
cold blue neon or purple neon signs
dry pavement — wet surface is mandatory
black night sky — must be blue hour indigo
master_prompt: Destination card image for rentatelo.com homepage — Miami as the iconic second-destination the LATAM family will drive to. Show Ocean Drive in South Beach, Miami Beach, Florida, at blue hour — 15 to 25 minutes after sunset. The Art Deco hotel facades from 5th to 10th Street fill the frame: Hotel Breakwater with activated vertical neon sign, Colony Hotel in pale yellow, Carlyle three-tower facade. Tall royal palms stand in the center median, their fronds catching warm neon color spill. The street is completely empty of people and moving traffic — one classic American car silhouette from the 1950s or 60s is parked at the curb, dark color, wet roof reflecting neon signs. The pavement is wet from recent rain, creating vivid horizontal neon reflections in pink, coral, and amber. Wide horizontal composition 4:3, buildings filling upper 70%, reflective pavement in lower 30%. Sky is deep indigo-purple post-sunset — fully blue hour, not black night. Neon signs activated: warm pink, red, amber tones at street level creating high contrast against the cool indigo sky above rooflines. 35mm editorial perspective, deep focus. Color: deep indigo sky, warm neon pink-amber from signs, teal 168F8A in facade shadows, amber F0B576 accent in neon warmth. Avoid: any people, moving vehicles, beach or ocean, generic skyline, dry pavement, cold blue neon, daytime light, aerial angle, text, watermarks.
